document.title: Guidelines for the Assessment of the Competence of Testing Laboratories Involved in the Import and Export Control of Food
document.label: CXG 27-1997 - Guidelines for the Assessment of the Competence of Testing Laboratories Involved in the Import and Export Control of Food

sections.title: ASSESSMENT BODIES
sections.text: 4. The bodies assessing the laboratories referred to above should comply with the general criteria for laboratory accreditation, such as those laid down in the ISO/IEC Guide 58:1993: “Calibration and testing laboratory accreditation systems - General requirements for operation and recognition.”

sections.title: REQUIREMENTS
sections.text: 3. The following quality criteria should be adopted by laboratories involved in the import and export control of foods: - Compliance with the general criteria for testing laboratories laid down in ISO/IEC Guide 17025:1999 “General requirements for the competence of calibration and testing laboratories”; - Participation in appropriate proficiency testing schemes for food analysis which conform to the requirements laid down in “The International Harmonized Protocol for the Proficiency Testing of (Chemical) Analytical Laboratories”, Pure & Appl. Chem. 78 (2006) 145-196; - Whenever available, use methods of analysis which have been validated according to the principles laid down by the Codex Alimentarius Commission; and - Use internal quality control procedures, such as those described in the “Harmonized Guidelines for Internal Quality Control in Analytical Chemistry Laboratories”, Pure & Appl. Chem. 67 (1995) 649-666.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: 1. These guidelines provide a framework for the implementation of quality assurance measures to ensure the competence of testing laboratories involved in the import and export control of foods. 2. These guidelines are intended to assist countries in the application of requirements for trade in foodstuffs in order to protect the consumers and to facilitate fair trade.

classification.pattern: Enumerated list introduced by modal verb 'should' + base verb phrase + multiple specific quality criteria

components.Subject: laboratories involved in the import and export control of foods
Quality criteria: Compliance with general criteria in ISO/IEC Guide 17025:1999
Participation in proficiency testing schemes conforming to international protocols
Use methods of analysis validated by Codex Alimentarius Commission
Use internal quality control procedures as described in guidelines

classification.justification: The sentence uses 'should' to impose an obligation to adopt multiple detailed quality criteria enumerated explicitly; this complex enumeration constitutes a new pattern not covered by known templates.
notes: Complex list structure with several normative references; obligation modality clearly present.

pages.title: GUIDELINES FOR THE ASSESSMENT OF THE COMPETENCE OF TESTING LABORATORIES INVOLVED IN THE IMPORT AND EXPORT CONTROL OF FOOD
pages.summary: Página 1 do documento CAC/GL 27-1997 acompanha principais seções SCOPE, REQUIREMENTS e início da seção ASSESSMENT BODIES. Seções são nível 1 por títulos em caixa alta. A seção REQUIREMENTS continua para a próxima página. Texto citado está limpo do cabeçalho repetitivo e estruturado para uso em RAG.
